page: Microsoft Azure D32as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dav6 server family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The instance features 32 dedicated virtual CPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with multithreading enabled, operating at a base frequency of 3.7 GHz. It is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. This configuration does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable, allowing connection to external high-performance block storage.

In benchmark evaluations, the D32as_v6 demonstrates strong single-core capabilities, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranking in the top tier (top 10%). Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores both fall within the strong top 25% tier. Memory performance is robust, highlighted by a strong PassMark memory score and efficient bzip3 decompression speeds. However, multi-core stress-ng performance and memory bandwidth for large blocks (both read and write) align with the average middle 50% of tested systems. For specialized tasks, the server achieves top-tier text generation speeds on small language models, though performance scales down to average levels for larger 7B and 70B parameter models.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the D32as_v6 offers a balanced resource density suitable for general-purpose enterprise applications without the premium cost of specialized memory-optimized or GPU-accelerated hardware. The absence of bundled local storage means storage costs are decoupled, allowing users to scale external storage independently. This instance is well-suited for workloads such as web serving, application hosting, software compilation, text and image processing, and moderate database operations.
description: Microsoft Azure D32as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dav6 family, powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 x86_64 processor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs (16 physical cores) running at 3.7 GHz and 128.0 GB of RAM, yielding a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-memory ratio. While it lacks local storage and GPUs, it is Premium Storage capable. Benchmarks reveal top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong Geekbench scores, alongside average multi-core and memory bandwidth results. This resource profile makes the D32as_v6 a cost-efficient option for general-purpose enterprise workloads, including web serving, software compilation, and text processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D32as_v6 is a general-purpose virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and top-tier single-core performance, ideal for enterprise applications and web serving.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D32as_v6 is a general-purpose VM with 32 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 128 GB RAM, delivering strong single-core and text processing performance.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and strong single-core performance for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 128 GB RAM.
Top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong Geekbench multi-core results.
Premium Storage capable configuration with no bundled local storage.
Optimized for general-purpose workloads, web serving, and software compilation.
Delivers strong performance in text processing and small-model LLM inference.
